title: Add per-agent-type model selection for Claude Code
description:
Claude Code currently uses whatever model is configured as the user's default (no --model flag is passed). Add configurable model selection per agent type.

CONFIG CHANGES (.orchestrator/config.yaml and config.py):
Add to the claude backend config:
- model_default: the default model for all agent types (default: 'claude-sonnet-4-6')
- model_by_agent: per-agent-type overrides merged with model_default, same pattern as allowed_tools_by_agent

Example config:
  claude:
    model_default: claude-sonnet-4-6
    model_by_agent:
      developer: claude-sonnet-4-6
      tester: claude-sonnet-4-6
      planner: claude-sonnet-4-6
      review: claude-sonnet-4-6
      documentation: claude-sonnet-4-6

Add a method to OrchestratorConfig: model_for(backend, agent_type) -> str | None that returns model_by_agent[agent_type] if set, else model_default, else None.

RUNNER CHANGES (runner.py):
In ClaudeCodeAgentRunner._exec_json(), resolve the model via self.config.model_for('claude', agent_type) and pass --model <model_id> to the claude command if not None. Also pass --model in _retry_structured_output(). The agent_type parameter already exists on _exec_json from the allowed_tools work.

For CodexAgentRunner, no changes needed (Codex model selection is handled differently).

DEFAULT VALUES: When no config file exists, default_config() should use claude-sonnet-4-6 for all agent types. The .orchestrator/config.yaml file should include the model_default and model_by_agent keys with these defaults.
acceptance_criteria: ClaudeCodeAgentRunner passes --model flag to claude CLI when model is configured
Model is resolved per agent type via config.model_for(backend, agent_type)
model_by_agent overrides model_default for specific agent types
default_config() uses claude-sonnet-4-6 for all agent types
.orchestrator/config.yaml includes model_default and model_by_agent with defaults
When model is None (no config), no --model flag is passed (uses Claude Code default)

handoff_summary.completed: All parallel test runs executed and passed.

All four parallel test runs passed successfully.

guardrails.template_text:
# Developer Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Implement only the assigned bead inside the existing architecture and file scope.

Allowed actions:
- Modify code, configuration, or local docs required to complete the assigned bead.
- Create sub-beads for discovered follow-up work that should be handled separately.
- Run only the specific test files directly related to your changes, not the full test suite. Use `uv run python -m unittest tests.<module_name> -v` to target individual test files rather than `discover`. Leave comprehensive test validation to the tester agent.

Disallowed actions:
- Redesign unrelated architecture or broaden scope beyond the assigned bead.
- Perform final review signoff in place of a review agent.
- Silently absorb unrelated test, documentation, or planning work that should be handed off.

Expected outputs:
- Completed or blocked JSON with concise implementation summary.
- Accurate touched files, changed files, risks, and follow-up handoff fields.
